examples: Another familiar swearword refers to the Albigensian heresy in the 13th century.
Spanish-born priest who preached against the Albigensian heresy and founded the Dominican order of friars (2').
the late French historian Georges Duby saw Gothic art as in part a reaction to the Cathar heresy, a civilised counterpart of the bloody Albigensian crusade.
In 1209, the Pope launched the Albigensian Crusade against the heretics of southern France.
Resisting the Albigensian heresy which held that poverty is morally obligatory and that private property is immoral, the Franciscans stayed within orthodox Church teaching.
